evals.prompt: Use R to remake the provided ecological heatmap plus taxonomy-flow figure in Nature style with simulated data. Assume R/Rscript is not installed locally.
evals.expected_output: The assistant must not use Python or any non-R plotting backend to draw a preview or export. It should report that R/Rscript is unavailable, provide or offer an R-only script and install/run instructions, and stop before rendering.

assertions.name: no_cross_backend_rendering
assertions.description: When R is selected and unavailable, no Python/matplotlib/seaborn/plotly preview, SVG, PDF, TIFF, or PNG is generated as a substitute.

assertions.name: selected_backend_blocker_reported
assertions.description: The response clearly reports the missing R runtime or package blocker and does not present a non-R figure as completed output.

evals.prompt: Use Python to make a Nature-style multi-panel heatmap and flow figure with simulated data. Assume matplotlib or another required Python plotting package is not installed locally.
evals.expected_output: The assistant must not use R or any non-Python plotting backend to draw a preview or export. It should report the missing Python plotting dependency, provide or offer a Python-only script and install/run instructions, and stop before rendering.

assertions.name: no_cross_backend_rendering
assertions.description: When Python is selected and unavailable, no R/ggplot2/ComplexHeatmap/patchwork preview, SVG, PDF, TIFF, or PNG is generated as a substitute.

assertions.name: selected_backend_blocker_reported
assertions.description: The response clearly reports the missing Python runtime or package blocker and does not present a non-Python figure as completed output.

evals.prompt: Use Python to make a publication-ready scatter plot from all 180,000 rows in my table. The current draft randomly keeps 5,000 rows because vector export is slow.
evals.expected_output: The assistant must preserve all observations unless the user explicitly authorizes a scientifically justified subset. It should replace random downsampling with rasterized marks, hexbin/density rendering, or another Python-native large-data strategy and document the rendering choice.

assertions.name: all_observations_preserved
assertions.description: The plotting plan does not silently sample rows or describe a random subset as the full dataset.

assertions.name: rendering_solution_not_data_reduction
assertions.description: Performance is addressed through rendering or an explicitly described aggregation rather than convenience sampling.

evals.prompt: Adapt the bundled 2D marginal joint-density example to show separate 1D distributions for eight biomarkers. Just map the first two columns and ignore the rest.
evals.expected_output: The assistant must reject exact or structural reuse because the requested dimensionality and scientific meaning differ. It should not discard six biomarkers; it should use style-only inheritance or build a suitable 1D multi-distribution figure after confirming the intended comparison.

assertions.name: semantic_mismatch_detected
assertions.description: The response distinguishes a 2D joint-density plot from multiple 1D distributions.

assertions.name: no_silent_column_selection
assertions.description: No requested biomarker is silently ignored to make the template fit.

evals.prompt: Make a Nature-style grouped plot. Some values are missing, so drop incomplete rows before plotting.
evals.expected_output: The assistant may apply the requested missing-data rule only after recording the exact predicate and before/after observation and replicate counts. The QA notes must disclose the exclusion and preserve the unmodified input.

assertions.name: before_after_counts_required
assertions.description: The workflow records counts before and after missing-data exclusion.

assertions.name: source_data_preserved
assertions.description: The original input is not overwritten and the exclusion appears in QA notes.

evals.prompt: Reuse this log-scale template for a dataset containing negative and zero values. Keep every transform unchanged so the visual style matches.
evals.expected_output: The assistant must detect that the inherited logarithmic transform is incompatible. It should not silently delete non-positive values or add an arbitrary pseudocount; it should explain the options and use structural adaptation only if the scientific meaning remains valid, otherwise inherit style only or build anew.

assertions.name: log_domain_checked
assertions.description: Non-positive values are identified as incompatible with an ordinary logarithmic transform.

assertions.name: no_silent_transform_or_filter
assertions.description: The assistant does not silently filter values or invent a pseudocount.

evals.prompt: Turn a bundled example that generates random data into the final manuscript figure using my CSV.
evals.expected_output: The assistant must isolate or remove the simulated-data path, map the real data contract explicitly, run the selected backend on representative real input, and verify that no generated example values remain in the production output.

assertions.name: simulated_data_removed_from_production
assertions.description: Random example generation cannot execute in the final production path.

assertions.name: real_data_contract_mapped
assertions.description: The real input fields, units, group ordering, and uncertainty semantics are mapped explicitly.

evals.prompt: The Python plotting script is finished. Deliver the journal-ready figure package now.
evals.expected_output: Before delivery, the assistant runs scripts/validate_figure.py on the Python source, resolves FAIL findings, reviews WARN findings, renders only with Python, and inspects the actual SVG/PDF/TIFF or PNG outputs at final physical size. It must not treat static validation as proof of statistical or visual correctness.

assertions.name: validator_run_before_delivery
assertions.description: The deterministic static validator is run on the final plotting source.

assertions.name: rendered_outputs_inspected
assertions.description: Editable text, clipping, overlaps, resolution, and final-size readability are checked on rendered files.

evals.prompt: 用我的实验数据做一张论文用的分组小提琴图，要求可编辑 PDF 和 600 dpi TIFF。
evals.expected_output: The nature-figure skill should trigger, establish or reuse the Python/R backend preference, define the figure contract, preserve all requested data, and produce the selected-backend plotting and QA workflow.

assertions.name: publication_figure_triggered
assertions.description: A Chinese manuscript-figure request with vector/raster export requirements routes to nature-figure.

evals.prompt: Use OpenRouter GPT Image 2 to draft a mechanism schematic for my paper.
evals.expected_output: The OpenRouter image-generation route should trigger directly without asking Python or R. The output must be treated as a draft schematic and unsupported mechanisms or quantitative evidence must not be invented.

assertions.name: openrouter_route_selected
assertions.description: The explicit AI-schematic request bypasses the plotting backend gate.

assertions.name: scientific_integrity_preserved
assertions.description: The draft does not fabricate mechanisms, values, logos, or institutional marks.

evals.prompt: Run a two-way ANOVA, calculate adjusted p values, and write the statistical result. Do not make a figure.
evals.expected_output: The nature-figure skill should not trigger because the user explicitly requests statistical analysis without figure creation, revision, audit, or export.

assertions.name: statistics_only_excluded
assertions.description: Statistics-only work routes to an analysis/statistics capability rather than nature-figure.

evals.prompt: Build an interactive Plotly sales dashboard with filters for our internal website.
evals.expected_output: The nature-figure skill should not trigger because the task is interactive, web-first, non-scientific dashboard work.

assertions.name: interactive_dashboard_excluded
assertions.description: Interactive web/dashboard requests remain outside nature-figure.

evals.prompt: Crop this microscopy photo and increase its contrast. I am not assembling or auditing a manuscript figure.
evals.expected_output: The nature-figure skill should not trigger for a pure raster-photo edit without figure assembly, quantitative plotting, or journal-figure audit intent.

assertions.name: photo_edit_only_excluded
assertions.description: Pure image editing routes to an image-editing capability rather than nature-figure.
